Container.Name: Hermes-Agent
Container.Overview: Hermes Agent is a self-improving personal AI agent by Nous Research. This Unraid template runs the official Docker image as a persistent gateway with optional dashboard, OpenAI-compatible API server, messaging gateway settings, and persistent appdata.
Container.Requires: Hermes dashboard is unauthenticated when published to the network. Keep it LAN/VPN/Tailscale only or place it behind a reverse proxy with authentication. Do not expose the dashboard or API server directly to the internet.

Config.Name: AppData
Config.Description: Persistent Hermes home directory. Stores .env, config.yaml, sessions, memories, skills, cron jobs, logs, and dashboard state.

Config.Name: Workspace
Config.Description: Writable workspace for files Hermes should be able to read and write. Point this at a specific share if you want Hermes to work on files outside appdata.

Config.Name: Gateway API Port
Config.Description: Container Port: 8642. OpenAI-compatible API and health endpoint. Requires API_SERVER_ENABLED=true to serve requests.

Config.Name: Dashboard Port
Config.Description: Container Port: 9119. Hermes web dashboard. Requires HERMES_DASHBOARD=1 and HERMES_DASHBOARD_HOST=0.0.0.0 to be reachable from Unraid.

Config.Name: Timezone
Config.Description: Timezone used by the container for logs and scheduled work.

Config.Name: Hermes UID
Config.Description: UID for the internal hermes user. Default 99 maps to Unraid nobody so appdata files are manageable from shares.

Config.Name: Hermes GID
Config.Description: GID for the internal hermes user. Default 100 maps to Unraid users.

Config.Name: Enable Dashboard
Config.Description: Set to 1 to launch the dashboard side-process in the same container as the gateway.

Config.Name: Dashboard Host
Config.Description: 0.0.0.0 is required for Unraid to reach the dashboard through the published port. Use 127.0.0.1 only for sidecar reverse proxy patterns.

Config.Name: Dashboard Port Inside Container
Config.Description: Dashboard listen port inside the container. Keep this matching the Dashboard Port container target unless you know you need a different value.

Config.Name: Enable Dashboard Chat Tab
Config.Description: Set to 1 to expose the dashboard's browser chat tab. Keep disabled when using HERMES_UID/HERMES_GID remapping unless your image version has the dashboard TUI permission fix.

Config.Name: Enable API Server
Config.Description: Enables the OpenAI-compatible API and health endpoint inside the container. Leave API_SERVER_HOST at 127.0.0.1 for dashboard/internal use; set API_SERVER_KEY before publishing to LAN.

Config.Name: API Server Host
Config.Description: Use 0.0.0.0 only when you intentionally want other containers or LAN clients to reach the API through the published port.

Config.Name: API Server Key
Config.Description: Bearer token for the OpenAI-compatible API. Required when exposing the API beyond container localhost. Use a strong random value.

Config.Name: API CORS Origins
Config.Description: Optional CORS origins for browser clients. Use a specific origin where possible; '*' is convenient but broad.

Config.Name: Default Model Override
Config.Description: Optional process-level model override. Prefer dashboard/config.yaml for normal model selection unless you want an environment override.

Config.Name: OpenRouter API Key
Config.Description: Optional OpenRouter API key. Useful as a first provider because Hermes supports many models through OpenRouter.

Config.Name: Anthropic API Key
Config.Description: Optional Anthropic API key.

Config.Name: OpenAI API Key
Config.Description: Optional OpenAI or OpenAI-compatible API key.

Config.Name: OpenAI Base URL
Config.Description: Optional OpenAI-compatible base URL. For another container on the same bridge network, use its container DNS name; for a host service on Linux, consider host networking or a reachable LAN address.

Config.Name: Google API Key
Config.Description: Optional Google/Gemini API key.

Config.Name: XAI API Key
Config.Description: Optional xAI API key.

Config.Name: NVIDIA API Key
Config.Description: Optional NVIDIA NIM API key.

Config.Name: Hugging Face Token
Config.Description: Optional Hugging Face token.

Config.Name: Telegram Bot Token
Config.Description: Optional Telegram bot token for the messaging gateway.

Config.Name: Telegram Allowed Users
Config.Description: Optional comma-separated Telegram user IDs allowed to talk to Hermes. Strongly recommended if Telegram is enabled.

Config.Name: Discord Bot Token
Config.Description: Optional Discord bot token for the messaging gateway.

Config.Name: Discord Allowed Users
Config.Description: Optional comma-separated Discord user IDs allowed to talk to Hermes.

Config.Name: Messaging Working Directory
Config.Description: Default working directory for messaging-originated tasks. The template mounts Workspace at /workspace.

Config.Name: Teams Port
Config.Description: Optional Microsoft Teams gateway port. Only set a host port if you configure the Teams gateway.

Config.Name: Teams Client ID
Config.Description: Optional Microsoft Teams bot client ID.

Config.Name: Teams Client Secret
Config.Description: Optional Microsoft Teams bot client secret.

Config.Name: Teams Tenant ID
Config.Description: Optional Microsoft Teams tenant ID.

Config.Name: Teams Allowed Users
Config.Description: Optional comma-separated Teams users allowed to talk to Hermes.

Config.Name: Google Chat Project ID
Config.Description: Optional Google Chat project ID.

Config.Name: Google Chat Subscription
Config.Description: Optional Google Chat Pub/Sub subscription name.

Config.Name: Google Chat Service Account JSON
Config.Description: Path inside the container to a mounted Google Chat service account JSON file, for example /secrets/google-chat-sa.json.

Config.Name: Google Chat Allowed Users
Config.Description: Optional comma-separated Google Chat users allowed to talk to Hermes.

Config.Name: Google Chat Service Account Mount
Config.Description: Optional host path to a Google Chat service account JSON file. Set GOOGLE_CHAT_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON to /secrets/google-chat-sa.json when using this.

Config.Name: Docker Socket
Config.Description: Optional and powerful. Mount /var/run/docker.sock only if you want Hermes to control host Docker from inside the container.

Config.Name: Host Share Mount
Config.Description: Optional host share for Hermes file access. Prefer a specific path like /mnt/user/projects instead of the whole array.
